checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully reproduces the text found in the source PDF for the sections it successfully converted. Metadata titles and alt-text for images (logos and overlays) are generated appropriately for accessibility purposes and are not considered fabrications under the guidelines.

I verified the phone numbers and names present in the roll call tables on pages 1, 2, and 10. The phone numbers '352-672-4039' and '919-869-4013' found on page 2 are correctly captured in the output JSON. No fabricated contact details or URLs were found.

The source PDF consists of 10 pages. A comparison between the source and the output JSON reveals that nearly all narrative content from pages 3 through 9 has been omitted. The output successfully captures the header and tables from pages 1 and 2, and then jumps immediately to the final tables and adjournment text on page 10. Entire sections such as the 'Executive Director Report', 'Education Committee Report', 'Funding Committee Report', 'Finance Team Report', 'Grant Committee Report', 'Standards Committee Report', and 'Technology Committee Report' (occupying pages 3-9) are completely absent.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains narrative text spanning pages 3 through 9, including headers such as '5. Executive Director Report', '7. Education Committee Report', '8. Funding Committee Report', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output JSON transitions from the Guest list table (page 2, reading_order 13) to the Roll Call Vote roster (page 10, reading_order 80), skipping all intermediate text.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections following the Guest list table, pages 3-9

checks.a_analysis: The tables that were successfully converted (the Board Member list on page 1, the Guest list on page 2, and the Roll Call Vote roster on page 10) have the correct number of rows and match the source data perfectly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The meeting minutes, which form the primary section of this document, are missing approximately 70% of their total content. While this is captured as a total page omission in C3, it also constitutes a critical failure of section-level fidelity as the bulk of the record of the meeting is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Narrative meeting minutes content across pages 3-9.
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire content of the meeting minutes (reports, remarks, and discussions) is omitted between the initial attendance list and the final vote tallies.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: For the portions of the document that were converted, the text is accurate. For example, the detailed titles and attendance statuses in the tables on pages 1 and 2 match the source exactly.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output matches the source PDF. Information flows linearly from the meeting header and roll call tables through the numbered minutes sections to the final voting roster. While the HTML includes some visual redundancy by embedding images of the original PDF pages, the overall organizational structure and visual flow of the information is preserved and intuitive.

All tables from the source document, including the roll call tables for members, staff, and guests, as well as the complex 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' grid at the end, are rendered correctly as HTML tables. They maintain clear row and column structures with visible borders and distinct headers. Although the voting roster table is horizontally wide due to text-heavy headers, the data remains aligned and readable.

Meaningful images, specifically the NC DIT logos found at the top and bottom of the source PDF, are successfully rendered in the HTML output. The red diagonal watermark present in the source is correctly omitted as a decorative element.

The HTML output contains significant visual duplication of body content. The roll call information (Board Members, Staff, and Guests) is first rendered as accessible HTML tables. Immediately following these tables, the document embeds large images of the original PDF pages 1 and 2, which contain the exact same roll call information. This results in the same body content block appearing twice in the document flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains roll call tables on pages 1 and 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output renders the roll call data as HTML tables and then immediately includes two images (screenshots of PDF page 1 and 2) containing the same tables.
checks.b_evidence_location: In the HTML body, immediately following the 'NC 911 Board Members' table and before the 'North Carolina 911 Board Meeting Roll Call' (Guests) section.
